commonMedications.name: Semaglutida / GLP-1 (Ozempic) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Not covered by CCSS typically. Cash price at private pharmacies.

commonMedications.name: Levotiroxina [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Available through CCSS or private pharmacies

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Farmacia Fischel, Farmacia La Bomba, Walmart pharmacy, and CCSS (Caja) pharmacies for enrolled members. Private pharmacies in most towns.
medicine.notes: Costa Rica CCSS (Caja) provides universal healthcare including medications for enrolled residents (~7-11% of income). Legal residents must enroll. CCSS formulary covers most generics at no additional cost. Private pharmacies for non-formulary drugs.

plans.name: Google Fi Flexible (US Number)
plans.notes: Line 1 $20 + Line 2 $15 = $35 base. Data $10/GB × 3GB = $30. Works in Costa Rica at same rate. Keeps US number for banking/2FA.

plans.name: Kolbi (ICE) Postpaid
plans.notes: 2 lines ~₡5,000/line (~$9). Kolbi is state telecom. Taxes included.

items.name: Ground Beef / Turkey
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Fish (local catch)
items.notes: Fresh local fish often cheaper than US

items.name: Coffee (ground/beans)
items.notes: Excellent local coffee in most LATAM countries

items.name: Premium Dog Food (kibble)
items.notes: Imported premium brands cost more; local brands available

breakdown.notes: Security deposit typically 2 months rent. Water/electricity separate.

housing.taxNotes: No VAT on residential rent. Property tax (Impuesto sobre Bienes Inmuebles) 0.25% annually, paid by owner.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month minimum lease (3-year implied term under Costa Rican law). Security deposit 1-2 months. Tenants have strong legal protections.
housing.marketNotes: Central Valley (Escazu, Santa Ana) most expensive. Guanacaste beach areas seasonal pricing. Furnished rentals common for expats.

healthcare.coverageSummary: CCSS (Caja) enrollment mandatory for residents, ~$80-150/mo based on income. Covers comprehensive care including dental and pharmacy. Private insurance optional.

gpVisit.notes: Caja covers GP visits fully. Wait times can be long for non-urgent care.

specialist.notes: Caja covers specialists but wait times 1-3 months. Private $60-100 per visit.

privateInsurance.notes: INS (national insurer) or international plans. Private supplements Caja for faster access.

emergency.notes: CCSS emergency care free for members. Private ER $300-600.

dental.notes: Caja covers basic dental. Private dental still much cheaper than US.

healthcare.notes: Costa Rica consistently ranks high in healthcare quality. CCSS provides universal coverage. Many US-trained doctors in private practice.

mensCut.notes: Barber shops and salons in all towns

gymMonthly.notes: MultiSpa, Gold's Gym available in Puerto Viejo, Costa Rica

massage60min.notes: Good spa options in tourist/expat areas

discounts.name: Ciudadano de Oro Card
discounts.description: Government senior card providing discounts on transportation, entertainment, and some services for residents 65+.

discounts.name: CCSS Healthcare
discounts.description: CCSS (Caja) provides comprehensive healthcare including dental and pharmacy for enrolled residents. Pensionado visa holders eligible.

discounts.name: Public Transit
discounts.description: Bus fare exemptions or reductions for Ciudadano de Oro cardholders on public buses.

discounts.name: Property Tax Exemption
discounts.description: Primary residence exemption from property tax for homes valued under certain thresholds.

discounts.name: Recreation & Culture
discounts.description: Reduced entry to national parks and cultural events. Free access to some public recreation programs.

monthlyPass.notes: No unified transit card. Cash payment on buses. Private bus companies operate most routes.

publicTransit.coverage: Bus networks in San Jose and major cities. No metro/rail system.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Ciudadano de Oro card for reduced bus fares for 65+.

rideShare.availability: Uber available but in legal gray area. DiDi growing. Red taxis regulated with meters.
rideShare.notes: Ride-share widely used in Rica Puerto Viejo. Negotiate taxi fares before riding if no meter.

registration.notes: Vehicle registration through local transit authority in Rica Puerto Viejo.

insurance.notes: Liability insurance mandatory. International driving permit accepted for first 90 days.

fuel.notes: Fuel prices vary by region. Government may regulate prices.

parking.notes: Street parking available in Rica Puerto Viejo. Malls and supermarkets usually have free parking.

tolls.notes: Toll roads on major highways between cities.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: No specific senior car discounts. Marchamo (annual vehicle tax) applies to all.

transportation.walkability: Moderate. Central Valley towns walkable. Beach/rural areas car-dependent.

cycling.infrastructure: Bicycle infrastructure varies in Rica Puerto Viejo. Urban areas gradually improving cycling lanes.
cycling.notes: Heat and terrain may limit cycling as primary transport.

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Costa Rica incentivizes EVs with tax exemptions. Limited but growing charging network. Import costs may apply.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Law 9518: EVs exempt from sales tax and selective consumption tax. Reduced import duties.

chargingHome.notes: Home charging feasible in houses and some apartments in Rica Puerto Viejo.

chargingPublic.notes: Public charging network growing in Costa Rica. Check availability in Rica Puerto Viejo.

insurance.notes: EV insurance through major local providers.

maintenance.notes: EV service availability growing. Authorized dealers in major cities.

registration.notes: Reduced or exempt registration for EVs.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: Manufacturer standard warranty. Service access best in major cities.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EV resale market developing in Costa Rica. Larger cities have better resale prospects.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging, insurance, maintenance (monthly avg), registration. Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.

eyeExam.notes: Visita oculistica ~30 EUR. SSN (public health) covers ophthalmologist with GP referral. Private ~30-50 EUR.

glasses.notes: Basic frames+lenses ~80 EUR at chains like GrandVision, Salmoiraghi. Progressive ~180 EUR. Not covered by SSN.

insurance.coverage: SSN covers ophthalmology referrals; glasses/contacts not covered
insurance.notes: No standalone vision insurance common. Private health (Unisalute, Generali) may include vision benefits.

cleaning.notes: Pulizia dentale ~50 EUR. Very limited public dental for adults.

filling.notes: Otturazione composita ~70 EUR. Private dental is standard; public waitlists are long.

crown.notes: Corona in ceramica ~400 EUR. Dental funds (fondi sanitari) through employers reduce costs.

insurance.coverage: Private dental plan or fondo sanitario, discounted cleanings and treatments
insurance.notes: Dental-only plans ~15 EUR/mo (DentaVox, Unisalute Dental). Free cleanings, 30-50% off treatments. Fondi sanitari via CCNL employment contracts common.

_seedOrigin.note: Template-derived section — prices are EUR-denominated from Puglia, Italy. Needs per-location currency + cost-of-living adaptation.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Pranzo (lunch) menus available €10-15 at trattorie. Coperto (cover charge) €1-3.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at trattoria ~€28 + coperto, 2x/week = ~€220/mo = ~$250/mo in Puglia, Italy.

categories.name: NFL RedZone
categories.notes: DAZN NFL Game Pass €19.99/mo during season. Annualized ~€13/mo (~$15).

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: Palestre comunali (municipal gyms) offer senior rates.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: EU citizens 65+ get free/reduced museum entry. Parks free.
categories.notes: Free parks, piazzas, coastal walks, hiking.

categories.name: Books/Media
categories.notes: Public libraries free. Budget for newspapers, books.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: No specific discounts. Espresso at the bar is very cheap.
categories.notes: Espresso €1-1.50 at the bar, cappuccino €1.50-2.00. Budget for 2 visits/week. ~$12/mo.

_seedOrigin.note: Template-derived section — prices are EUR-denominated from Puglia, Italy. Needs per-location currency + cost-of-living adaptation.
